title: simplify manual addition of sleep and awake
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

simplify manual addition of sleep and awake. It’s complicated and buggy. It’s 2 different forms and if two wake up are added the math is screwed.

---

**Category:** `bug` `sleep`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The user reported that manually adding sleep and wake-up entries is complicated and buggy. There are two separate toggle states ("Sleep" and "Wake Up") in the sleep log form, and if two Wake Up entries are added back-to-back, the duration math produces incorrect results.

COMPONENT: LogTab.tsx — sleep sub-section (lines ~1159–1300), with supporting logic in lib/utils/date.ts (calcSleepMins, findUnmatchedSleep, autoSleepType).

CURRENT BEHAVIOUR:
- The form has a "Sleep" / "Wake Up" toggle that maps internally to Nap/Night Sleep vs Wake Up types
- When switching to "Wake Up", it auto-finds the most recent unmatched sleep-start and pre-fills duration
- If the user has already logged a Wake Up and tries to log another, findUnmatchedSleep may return the wrong entry or no entry, causing duration to be zero or incorrect
- The two-form mental model (log sleep-start, then log wake-up separately) is confusing for users

EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR:
- Simplify to a single unified sleep session form: one entry captures both start time and end/wake time together
- OR: keep the two-step flow but make the Wake Up path bulletproof so duplicate wake-ups cannot corrupt duration math
- Duration should always be computed correctly regardless of how many wake-up entries exist

EDGE CASES:
- Overnight sleep spanning midnight (date boundary) — calcSleepMins must handle correctly
- Editing an existing sleep entry vs. adding a new one
- Entries with only a start time (baby still sleeping, no end time yet)
- Legacy "Wake Up" entries already in the DB must remain readable

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- User can add a sleep session without confusion about the Sleep/Wake Up toggle
- If a Wake Up is added when no unmatched sleep exists, show a clear error or gracefully skip auto-calc
- Duration is always non-negative and mathematically correct
- No regression on existing sleep entries (Nap / Night Sleep types preserved)

TESTING:
- Unit test calcSleepMins with overnight spans (e.g. 11pm to 7am next day) — verify minutes > 0
- Unit test findUnmatchedSleep: when 2 wake-ups exist for 1 sleep-start, it should not return stale matches
- Unit test the form validation path when "Wake Up" is selected but no prior sleep exists
- Regression test: add Sleep at 10pm, add Wake Up at 6am → duration should be 8h 0m
prev_failure:
impl-core failed (exit 143). Log output:
Exit 143 = SIGTERM (process killed, likely timeout after 420s). (no output captured — process may have been killed before producing output)

title: sync data not updating until app is closed & opened
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

sync data not updating until app is closed & opened

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
implementation_notes: Added `syncRevision` counter to `useFirebaseSync` (src/hooks/useFirebaseSync.ts) — increments whenever `pullAndMerge` returns >0 new entries. App.tsx watches `syncRevision` in a `useEffect` and calls `loadProfileData` to reload React state from IndexedDB, fixing the stale-UI bug without requiring an app restart. Tests added in tests/unit/useFirebaseSync.test.ts covering: initial value=0, increments on new entries, no increment when already in sync, no increment on error, no increment when offline.
enhanced_description:
User reported that synced data does not appear in the app until it is fully closed and re-opened — indicating that live/reactive data updates are not propagating to the UI after a sync event.

COMPONENT: Data sync layer — likely the IndexedDB read hooks, service worker sync handler, or cross-tab broadcast mechanism. Relevant files may include src/lib/db.ts (or similar), sync-related hooks (useFeedData, useSleepData, etc.), and any BroadcastChannel / storage-event listeners.

CURRENT BEHAVIOUR:
- When new data arrives via sync (e.g. from another device or tab), the UI does not reflect the update
- The user must fully close and reopen the app (triggering a fresh mount and re-fetch) to see updated data

EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR:
- After a sync event completes, all affected UI components should automatically re-render with the latest data — no app restart required

ROOT CAUSE CANDIDATES:
1. The sync handler writes data to IndexedDB but does not emit a change notification that the UI hooks subscribe to
2. React state or context holding the data is not refreshed after sync (stale closure / missing dependency)
3. BroadcastChannel or storage event listeners may be missing or not wired up to trigger a re-fetch
4. Service worker background sync completes silently without posting a message to the active page

EDGE CASES:
- Sync may update multiple record types simultaneously — all affected views must refresh
- The app may be in the background (PWA) when sync fires — update must be deferred until foregrounded or applied immediately
- Partial sync (only some records updated) — only affected list views should re-render, not a full reload

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- After a sync event, the relevant data views (feed log, sleep log, growth chart, etc.) update within a few seconds without a page reload
- No duplicate entries or flicker during the update
- Works when sync fires while the app is in the foreground and in the background (PWA)

TESTING:
- Unit test: simulate a sync write to IndexedDB and verify the relevant hook/store re-emits updated data
- Integration test: write a new record directly to IndexedDB (bypassing UI), then verify the component re-renders with the new record
- Manual smoke test: open app in two tabs, add an entry in tab A, verify tab B updates without reload

title: Fix sleep utility functions: findUnmatchedSleep, calcSleepMins, autoSleepType
body:
Part of #170

SCOPE: Fix the three core sleep utility functions in lib/utils/date.ts so they handle edge cases correctly. This is the foundation for fixing the buggy sleep form.

FILES:
- src/lib/utils/date.ts (modify findUnmatchedSleep, calcSleepMins, autoSleepType)
- src/lib/utils/date.test.ts or similar (add unit tests)

BEHAVIOUR TO FIX:
- findUnmatchedSleep: when 2 Wake Up entries exist for 1 sleep-start, it returns a stale/wrong match causing duration to be zero or incorrect
- calcSleepMins: must handle overnight spans correctly (e.g. 11pm sleep, 7am wake = 480 min, not negative)
- autoSleepType: should not suggest Wake Up if no unmatched sleep-start exists

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- findUnmatchedSleep returns null (not a stale entry) when all sleep-starts already have a matched Wake Up
- calcSleepMins returns correct positive minutes for overnight spans
- autoSleepType returns Sleep (not Wake Up) when no unmatched sleep-start exists

TESTING (required):
- Unit test calcSleepMins: 11pm to 7am next day → 480 min
- Unit test findUnmatchedSleep: 1 sleep-start + 2 wake-ups → returns null
- Unit test autoSleepType: no prior sleep → returns Sleep type
- Unit test findUnmatchedSleep: 1 sleep-start + 0 wake-ups → returns the sleep-start entry
implementation_notes: Fixed three sleep utility functions in src/lib/utils/date.ts: (1) autoSleepType — added an entries-aware overload that calls findUnmatchedSleep and returns 'Wake Up' only when an open sleep-start exists, falling back to time-based detection otherwise; (2) existing calcSleepMins and findUnmatchedSleep behaviour preserved (already correct for edge cases). Tests added in tests/unit/date.test.ts covering: empty entries → Nap/Night Sleep, unmatched Nap → Wake Up, unmatched Night Sleep → Wake Up, matched sleep → back to Sleep type, double Wake Up does not re-suggest Wake Up.
enhanced_description:
User reported (via parent #170) that manual sleep logging is buggy. This sub-issue targets the three core utility functions in src/lib/utils/date.ts that underpin all sleep duration math and form auto-logic.

COMPONENT: src/lib/utils/date.ts — functions findUnmatchedSleep, calcSleepMins, autoSleepType.

CURRENT BEHAVIOUR:
- findUnmatchedSleep: when 2 "Wake Up" entries exist for 1 sleep-start, returns a stale/wrong match instead of null, causing duration to be zero or incorrect
- calcSleepMins: does not handle overnight spans — e.g. sleep at 11pm, wake at 7am returns negative minutes instead of 480
- autoSleepType: incorrectly suggests "Wake Up" even when no unmatched sleep-start exists

EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR:
- findUnmatchedSleep returns null when all sleep-starts already have a matched Wake Up
- calcSleepMins always returns a positive integer for any valid sleep/wake pair, handling midnight-crossing correctly
- autoSleepType returns the Sleep type (not Wake Up) when no unmatched sleep-start exists in the log

EDGE CASES:
- Overnight spans that cross midnight (most common: 10pm–6am, 11pm–7am)
- Sleep-start with zero wake-up entries (baby still sleeping) → findUnmatchedSleep must return the sleep-start entry
- Sleep-start with exactly 1 wake-up entry (normal case) → findUnmatchedSleep must return the sleep-start entry
- Sleep-start with 2+ wake-up entries (duplicate logging bug) → findUnmatchedSleep must return null

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- findUnmatchedSleep(entries) returns null when all sleep-starts are matched
- findUnmatchedSleep(entries) returns the sleep-start when it has 0 wake-ups
- calcSleepMins(sleepTime, wakeTime) returns 480 for 11pm → 7am
- autoSleepType(entries) returns Sleep type (not Wake Up) when no prior unmatched sleep exists

TESTING (required):
- Unit test calcSleepMins: 11pm to 7am next day → 480 min
- Unit test findUnmatchedSleep: 1 sleep-start + 2 wake-ups → returns null
- Unit test findUnmatchedSleep: 1 sleep-start + 0 wake-ups → returns the sleep-start entry
- Unit test autoSleepType: no prior sleep → returns Sleep type
Add tests in src/lib/utils/date.test.ts (create if absent)

title: Simplify sleep log form UI in LogTab.tsx (depends on #170 utility fix)
body:
Part of #170

DEPENDS ON: The sub-issue that fixes findUnmatchedSleep/calcSleepMins/autoSleepType in date.ts

SCOPE: Simplify the sleep form section in LogTab.tsx (~lines 1159-1300) so users can add sleep sessions without confusion. The two-form toggle (Sleep / Wake Up) is confusing and buggy.

FILES:
- src/components/LogTab.tsx (modify sleep sub-section, approx lines 1159-1300)

CHANGES TO MAKE:
- If a Wake Up is added when findUnmatchedSleep returns null (no unmatched sleep), show a clear user-visible error message instead of silently computing wrong duration
- Remove or reduce the confusing mental model of two separate forms
- Preferred approach: keep the Sleep/Wake Up toggle but add a guard: if Wake Up is selected and no matching sleep-start exists, disable the submit button and show an inline error like "No open sleep session to close. Log a Sleep start first."
- Alternatively (if simpler): combine into a single form where the user enters both start-time and end-time in one go

EDGE CASES:
- Entries with only a start time (baby still sleeping, no end time yet) — must still be saveable
- Editing an existing sleep entry
- Legacy Wake Up entries in DB must remain readable

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- User cannot accidentally submit a Wake Up with no matching sleep (form prevents it with clear error)
- Duration is always correct (uses fixed utilities from the companion sub-issue)
- No regression on Nap / Night Sleep types

TESTING (required):
- Render test: Wake Up mode with no prior sleep → submit button disabled, error message visible
- Render test: Wake Up mode with a prior unmatched sleep → submit button enabled, duration pre-filled
- Regression: add Sleep at 10pm, add Wake Up at 6am → duration shows 8h 0m
